Shaal Hero Review: The Golden Construct Guide - Empires & Puzzles

Is Shaal still a divine protector or just a rusty robot? We analyze the Golden Construct's unique blend of damage, healing, and anti-dark mechanics in the current meta. Discover the best emblem paths and team synergies for this Covenant hero.
Shaal Hero Review: The Golden Construct
Shaal: The Golden Construct – Divine Repairs & Dark Protection

Shaal, the 5-star Holy hero from the Construct family, arrived in late 2023 as a "do-it-all" answer to the stagnant formations of the time. Part damage dealer, part healer, and full-time cleaner, he was designed to be the Swiss Army Knife of yellow heroes. While he doesn't have a costume yet, his base form still brings a unique mechanic to the table that can completely neutralize Dark teams.
If Marvel's Celestials and a clockwork robot had a baby, it would look like Shaal.

Shaal is a visual standout. He looks like a divine entity trapped in a golden exoskeleton, radiating "do not touch me" energy. The design screams "Construct," but with a holy, celestial twist that separates him from his grimy, industrial cousins like Brimstone. It's majestic, slightly intimidating, and very shiny.

Healing allies for 20% of the damage dealt is the chef's kiss. If he hits 5 enemies for roughly 500 damage each (2500 total), he heals everyone for 500 HP. It rewards you for buffering his attack or hitting defense-down enemies.
"All Holy allies receive all strong damage as regular damage." This is his signature move. It means

against Shaal and his yellow friends. It essentially turns a mono-Purple attacker's strength into a neutral hit.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Construct family bonus isn't the main draw here, but it's nice if you run him with
This is huge. It charges up as he takes damage. When he fires, he can boost everyone's health by up to 25%. This transforms him from a "healer" to a "tanky overhealer."
He cannot be Mindless Attacked or Silenced by special skills (mostly). This makes him very reliable against controllers like Miki, Peters, or the myriad of Silencers.
Note: He can still be stopped by mana reduction.
At the start of battle, he gets +20% defense for 6 turns. This is arguably the best Aether power for a tank/flank, ensuring he survives the opening tile cascade to fire his skill.

Upon release, Shaal was considered "broken" by many. The combination of high AoE damage, healing, and cleansing at Average speed felt like too much utility in one card. He was a staple in War Defenses.
Today, he is no longer the terrifying monster he was. Newer heroes hit harder and faster. However, his utility remains top-tier. A Cleanse + Heal combo on a hitter is rare and valuable.
He is excellent in Rush Wars and still very strong in standard PvP offense. He has transitioned from a "Must-Have Tank" to a "Luxury Support/Attacker."

If you pulled Shaal, celebrate! He is an account-changing hero. Prioritize leveling him immediately. He can function as your main tank and your main healer for map stages. Emblem him for Attack to get the most out of his fun mechanics.

Shaal is still worth the ascension mats, but think twice about the Alpha Aethers (LB2) if you have the latest "Astral" or "Slime" gods. He performs 90% of his job at LB1. Use him in your 2nd or 3rd War team or specifically to counter heavy ailment defenses.
"This bot which looks like Celestial from MCU is nasty."
- A common sentiment upon his release regarding his art and power.
"From 200% to 340% damage to all… at average… This is becoming ridiculous."
- Early forum reactions to his buff before release.
"He’s the GOAT and revive the Holy Tank Meta??!"
- YouTube titles were very optimistic about his tanking potential.

Shaal is the golden standard for "utility damage dealers." He might not hit the hardest anymore, and his paint might be chipping compared to the shiny new toys, but his ability to flip a losing board (cleansing everything and healing everyone) is timeless. He is a Construct built to last.
A divine addition to any roster, even years later.
